title: ContractFloorResult
description: Set 068 S5 - the raw result of running a set's contract command (declared in contract-manifest.json) in the Set 068 S1 disposable run_test cage. Written as contract-floor-result.json beside spec.md by the producer (python -m ai_router.contract_gate run). Records the RAW exit code + captured output (deterministic-servant discipline - never summarized). The close-out gate confirms a valid result of this shape exists, that its sessionSetName matches the set and its command matches the manifest, and that the floor PASSED (ran AND exitCode == 0 AND not timedOut AND the worktree was torn down). This JSON Schema is the STRUCTURAL contract; the runtime gate uses ai_router.contract_gate.validate_contract_floor_result, which additionally requires the recorded 'passed' flag to AGREE with that derived criterion. See docs/contract-gate.md.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope schema version. 1 = the v1 contract locked in Set 068 S5.

sessionSetName.description: The slug of the session set this floor result covers. The close-out gate confirms it matches the set being closed.

contractGate.description: The policy level under which the floor was produced.

ref.description: The git ref the cage checked a disposable worktree out from (provenance).

command.description: The resolved contract argv that actually ran (must match the manifest command).

ran.description: True iff the cage was created AND the command executed (NOT whether tests passed).

passed.description: The floor pass flag: ran AND exitCode == 0 AND not timedOut AND worktreeRemoved. Must agree with that derived criterion (the runtime validator rejects a mismatch).

exitCode.description: The command's raw exit code; null if it was killed (timeout).

timedOut.description: True iff the cage wall-clock cap fired and the process was killed.

worktreeCreated.description: Cage-lifecycle provenance: the disposable worktree was created.

worktreeRemoved.description: Cage-lifecycle provenance: the disposable worktree was fully torn down (a surviving registration is a leak and fails the floor).

producedAt.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp recording when the floor was produced.

output.description: The raw combined stdout+stderr (capped/elided), or the raw cage error when the command did not run. Never paraphrased.
